design_philosophy.core_concept: Digital Tactility - Interfaces that feel physical but look impossible.

scale.h1: text-5xl md:text-7xl font-bold tracking-tighter uppercase glow-text
scale.label: text-xs font-mono tracking-[0.2em] text-muted-foreground uppercase

layout_strategy.spacing: Dense but separated by distinct 'panel' borders.

turntable.description: The Hero Element. A realistic CSS/SVG composed vinyl record.
features: Spins when playing (use framer-motion animate={{ rotate: 360 }})
Tonearm animation (moves to record edge on play)
Center label displays station art or generic techno logo

visualizer.description: Real-time frequency analysis using HTML5 Canvas.
visualizer.placement: Under the turntable or integrated into the 'Mixer' section.

knob.visuals: Glowing ring indicating value. Tick marks around the outside.

station_browser.style: Data Terminal look. Monospace list items. Hover effects scanlines.

tech_stack_instructions.audio: Use standard HTML5 Audio API + Web Audio API (AudioContext) for visualization.

universal_guidelines: NEVER use generic gradients. Use 'noise' textures or distinct solid panels.
All interactive elements must have a :hover state that brightens or glows.
Dark Mode is the ONLY mode.
instructions_to_main_agent: 2. CREATE a 'useAudio' hook to handle the AudioContext, AnalyserNode, and HTMLAudioElement source.
3. IMPLEMENT the 'Turntable' component using pure CSS for the record grooves and Framer Motion for the spin.
4. BUILD the 'Knob' component. It must be functional (draggable).
5. FETCH radio stations using the Radio Browser API. Filter for 'Hip Hop', 'RnB', 'Trap'.
6. CONNECT the audio data to a Canvas element for the visualizer.
7. STYLE everything with the 'CyberDeck' aesthetic defined in colors/typography.
8. ENSURE the UI feels 'heavy' and 'expensive' (slow transitions, detailed borders).
